content.text:
{"id":"ca92ff28-607b-4f65-aad6-a4666801e942","identifier":"FRG-244","title":"Characters face toward objects when interacting","description":"## Problem

When a character arrives at an object, they keep whatever facing direction they had from walking. Sol uses the kotatsu but faces left instead of toward it.

## Root Cause

\_handleObjectArrival in Engine.js calls char.startInteraction() but never sets char.facing based on the object position.

## What to do

1. In \_handleObjectArrival, calculate facing direction from character position to object tile
2. Set char.facing before calling startInteraction()
3. Same for \_startConversation — characters should face each other

## Acceptance Criteria

* Character faces the object they are interacting with
* Characters face each other during conversations
* Facing updates immediately on interaction start","projectMilestone":{"id":"a6eb53b7-72b4-453e-9e23-aa42a629b7a4","name":"M2: Visual sim (main page)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-244/characters-face-toward-objects-when-interacting","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-244-characters-face-toward-objects-when-interacting","createdAt":"2026-03-09T04:29:22.998Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-09T08:17:09.781Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Dev in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.thinking: There's no `_startConversation` method, no conversation system. Let me check the AI engine to see if it has conversation logic.

Now I understand the codebase. Let me analyze what's missing and what needs to be implemented:

1. **Object facing in `_doInteraction`** - Already implemented! Lines 301-310 of engine.js already calculate the facing direction from character to object and set it. So this part is done.

2. **Character conversation facing** - In `ai.js`, the `talk` action (lines 171-208) moves a character toward the target character, but when they arrive, there's no facing logic. The character moves to an adjacent tile but never faces the target character.

Looking at the `talk` case:


The code moves the character toward the target if they're more than 2 tiles away, but once they're close enough (dist <= 2), nothing happens to make them face each other. I need to add facing logic that calculates the direction between the two characters and sets their facing when they're adjacent. I see the issue now—when characters move toward each other for conversations, the `targetObjectId` stays null since they're targeting another character, not an object, so the facing logic in `_doInteraction` never triggers. The issue description mentions methods like `_handleObjectArrival` and `_startConversation` that don't exist in the current codebase, which means I need to figure out where the facing logic should actually be implemented.

Looking at the existing facing code in `_doInteraction`, it calculates the direction from the character to the object's center and updates facing accordingly—this part seems to already be working for objects. But for character-to-character interactions, there's no equivalent facing setup happening when they arrive at their destination. there. So either the issue was created before this code was added, or there's still a conversation-facing problem. Looking at the git history, the most recent commit doesn't seem related to the facing fix, so let me dig deeper into how `_doInteraction` and the `talk` action actually work.

I see that `_doInteraction` handles facing, but when characters talk to each other in `ai.js`, there's a gap: if they're close enough (dist <= 2), they talk without moving and never face the target. If they move closer (dist > 2), they arrive at an adjacent tile but `targetObjectId` is never set for character-to-character conversations, so `_doInteraction` never gets called to update facing. The acceptance criteria clearly states characters should face each other during conversations, and that's not happening.

I need to fix this by either adding a `targetCharId` field to track character-to-character interactions separately, or handling the facing update differently in the `talk` action logic itself. When the character is already close enough, I can call `_updateFacing` directly with the direction toward the target. If they need to move first, I should set a `pendingFaceCharId` property that triggers facing behavior once they arrive at their destination, similar to how `pendingAction` works in the existing character update logic. I need to make sure character-to-character interactions have the facing logic too — when two characters talk, they should face each other, and this should happen immediately when the conversation starts, not just when they arrive. Let me verify what's already working: objects get the facing update in `_doInteraction`, but for characters I'm missing that piece. The real gap is making sure both participants in a conversation orient toward each other. Does the object facing code in `_doInteraction` already cover the case where a character is "already adjacent" (atTarget case in `_wanderTick`)? Let me trace through this: when a character reaches their target tile in `_wanderTick`, it calls `_doInteraction` which handles the facing direction. And in `_updateCharacters`, when a character finishes walking to an object, `_doInteraction` gets called again to set the facing. So object facing is fully covered.

For conversation facing in `ai.js`, the `talk` case handles it in two scenarios: if the target is already close (within 2 tiles), the character faces them immediately, otherwise they face toward the target once they arrive. Now I need to check whether the `talk` case sets `char.targetObjectId` and trace through the pathfinding logic...

Checking the talk case implementation...

Setting up character-to-character facing by adding a `targetCharId` field and updating the `talk` case to handle mutual facing when characters are close or when they arrive at their destination. Setting up mutual facing logic...

I need to make sure `targetCharId` gets cleared when a character starts moving toward an object, so it doesn't conflict with `targetObjectId`. Looking at the flow, when `moveTo` is called for an object target, I should clear any existing `targetCharId` to prevent the facing logic from triggering incorrectly when the character arrives. Still writing interaction logic... Still writing interaction logic... The simplest approach is to clear `targetCharId` whenever `targetObjectId` gets set, which would happen in the wandering logic and the AI interaction case. Alternatively, I could handle both arrival scenarios in `_updateCharacters` and unconditionally clear `targetCharId` after processing, whether it's an object interaction or a character interaction.

Implementing the character-facing feature now...

Writing the implementation... Writing the talk interaction logic...

Now I need to check what tests already exist and whether I should update or add new ones for this interaction system.

input.command: git add src/characters.js src/engine.js src/ai.js && git diff --staged

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
FRG-244: characters face toward objects and each other when interacting

Object-facing was already handled in _doInteraction. This adds the missing
conversation facing: when a character talks to another, both face each other
immediately if nearby, or on arrival after moving.

- characters.js: add targetCharId field for pending character-facing on arrival
- engine.js: handle targetCharId in _updateCharacters arrival check
- ai.js: set targetCharId when moving toward talk target; face immediately when close

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "FRG-244: Characters face toward objects and each other when interacting" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Object-facing on interaction was already in `_doInteraction` (lines 300-310 of engine.js) — this was already correct
- Added missing conversation facing: when AI characters use the `talk` action, they now face toward each other
- Both the initiator and the target character update their facing direction

## Changes
- **`src/characters.js`**: Added `targetCharId` field to Character for tracking a pending character-to-face on arrival
- **`src/engine.js`**: Extended `_updateCharacters` to handle `targetCharId` alongside the existing `targetObjectId` — when a character arrives after moving toward another for conversation, both characters face each other
- **`src/ai.js`**: In the `talk` case, face characters toward each other immediately if already within range (dist ≤ 2), or set `char.targetCharId` when moving so facing is applied on arrival

## Test plan
- [x] All 44 existing object state machine tests pass (`node --experimental-vm-modules tests/objects.test.js`)
- [ ] Manual verify: character facing toward interacted object (already working pre-PR via `_doInteraction`)
- [ ] Manual verify: two characters face each other when one initiates a talk action nearby
- [ ] Manual verify: two characters face each other when one moves to reach the other for conversation

Fixes: FRG-244

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
{"id":"ca92ff28-607b-4f65-aad6-a4666801e942","identifier":"FRG-244","title":"Characters face toward objects when interacting","description":"## Problem

When a character arrives at an object, they keep whatever facing direction they had from walking. Sol uses the kotatsu but faces left instead of toward it.

## Root Cause

\_handleObjectArrival in Engine.js calls char.startInteraction() but never sets char.facing based on the object position.

## What to do

1. In \_handleObjectArrival, calculate facing direction from character position to object tile
2. Set char.facing before calling startInteraction()
3. Same for \_startConversation — characters should face each other

## Acceptance Criteria

* Character faces the object they are interacting with
* Characters face each other during conversations
* Facing updates immediately on interaction start","projectMilestone":{"id":"a6eb53b7-72b4-453e-9e23-aa42a629b7a4","name":"M2: Visual sim (main page)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-244/characters-face-toward-objects-when-interacting","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-244-characters-face-toward-objects-when-interacting","createdAt":"2026-03-09T04:29:22.998Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-09T08:20:47.165Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Test in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"331aa66c-15ca-44b8-aa4a-457704a6e016","title":"FRG-244: Characters face toward objects and each other when interacting","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/3"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- **`src/characters.js`**: Added `targetCharId = null` field to Character constructor — tracks which character to face toward upon arrival
- **`src/engine.js`**: Refactored `_updateCharacters` arrival check to handle both `targetObjectId` (object interaction, already had facing via `_doInteraction`) and new `targetCharId` (conversation — faces both characters toward each other on arrival)
- **`src/ai.js`**: Updated `talk` case to face both characters toward each other immediately when already close (dist ≤ 2), or set `char.targetCharId` when moving toward the talk target so facing is applied on arrival

## Key Decisions
- Object-facing was already implemented in `_doInteraction` (the issue description referenced method names from an older version of the code) — no changes needed there
- The conversation facing applies to both the initiating character and the target, covering the "face each other" acceptance criterion
- `targetCharId` is cleared after use to avoid stale state; it takes effect only when `targetObjectId` is null, so object interactions naturally take priority

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/frg-244-characters-face-toward-objects-when-interacting`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/3
